title: CSS for Web Performance Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on optimizing CSS for faster web applications.

questions.question: What is the primary reason why CSS is considered a render-blocking resource?
options: It must be fully loaded before the DOM starts parsing.
The browser must construct the CSSOM before it can create the Render Tree.
It prevents JavaScript from executing until it is loaded.
It always requires a network request to load.

questions.question: Which of these CSS selector types is generally the most efficient for the browser to match?
options: Deeply nested selectors (e.g., body div ul li a)
Overly broad selectors (e.g., div *)
Direct class selectors (e.g., .nav-link)
Attribute selectors (e.g., [data-item='active'])

questions.question: What is 'Critical CSS'?
options: The CSS required to styles the entire page before any user interaction.
A CSS file that is only loaded on mobile devices.
The minimum CSS needed to render the 'above-the-fold' content, often inlined.
The CSS used to handle animations and transitions.

questions.question: Which property is handled by the browser's compositor and does not trigger a layout reflow?
options: width
margin
transform
padding

questions.question: What does the 'content-visibility: auto' property do?
options: It makes all off-screen elements invisible to screen readers.
It skips rendering work for off-screen elements until they are needed.
It forces the browser to render all elements immediately.
It prevents CSS from affecting the 'display' property of elements.
